outcome_contract.why: When staff check "Mark city tax as paid" during an extension, they expect the payment to be recorded. For guests without an outstanding balance (the majority, since most guests pay at check-in), nothing is written. Cash gets collected but never appears in the records — creating a reconciliation gap.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Checking "Mark city tax as paid" during an extension: (a) displays the correct collection amount including any old outstanding balance plus the extension nights' tax; and (b) always creates or updates a city tax record that correctly includes the extension nights' tax amount in `totalDue`, regardless of the guest's prior city tax status (outstanding balance, fully paid, or no prior record).

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Separate per-case display fix with branching

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: display and write use same formula, no UX mismatch

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same — one per occupant, but within 3 separate branches

stage_payload.decision_summary: The "Mark city tax as paid" checkbox in the extension modal has two bugs: (1) it displays the wrong collection amount for guests who have an outstanding city tax balance (shows only the old unpaid amount, not old amount + extension nights), and (2) it silently fails to write a city tax record for all three guest cases: outstanding balance, fully paid, and no prior record. The decision is which implementation approach to use for the fix. This is a single-component fix with no architectural ambiguity. The analysis resolves which of three plausible approaches is cleanest given the existing data m
goals: Fix `displayedCityTaxTotal` so staff see the correct amount to collect in all three cases
Fix `handleExtend` city tax block so a `CityTaxRecord` is always written when the checkbox is checked
Validate the fix via updated and new unit tests
non_goals: Changing the city tax rate (tracked as EXT-011)
Adding `allFinancialTransactions` mirroring for extension city tax
Replacing the checkbox with the full `CityTaxPaymentButton` component
constraints: Firebase `update()` is the only write primitive available — no RTDB transactions
assumptions: €2.50/night is the correct rate for this season
Extension city tax accumulates onto the existing record, not a separate node

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: All three cases must be handled — no conditional gaps
Single clear write path reduces future bug surface
Existing tests must accurately reflect new behaviour
Runtime changes confined to `ExtensionPayModal.tsx`; test changes in `ExtensionPayModal.test.tsx`
Must not introduce accumulation logic that conflicts with `CityTaxRecord` semantics

options_considered.Description: Replace both the display useMemo and the `if (record && record.balance > 0)` guard with a single unconditional write using `{ totalDue: (record?.totalDue??0)+ext, totalPaid: (record?.totalDue??0)+ext, balance: 0 }`
options_considered.Upside: Handles all 3 cases in one code path; no branching; display matches write exactly
options_considered.Key risks: None — test assertions are fully known

options_considered.Description: Keep case A branch for balance>0 (fixing its write shape), add `else if (record && record.balance === 0)` for case B, and `else` for case C
options_considered.Upside: Preserves original guard intent; explicit case labels
options_considered.Downside: 3× the code; harder to maintain; future bug surface is larger; display fix still needed separately
options_considered.Key risks: Divergence between display and write logic if maintained separately

options_considered.Description: Rewrite checkbox to use `useCityTaxPayment` hook pattern
options_considered.Downside: Requires increasing `totalDue` before calling helper; two distinct writes; would likely require `allFinancialTransactions` mirroring (rejected non-goal); significantly expands scope
options_considered.Key risks: Out-of-scope scope expansion; helper drives `balance` negative for fully-paid records if `totalDue` not pre-incremented

chosen_approach.Why this wins: All three guest cases collapse into a single formula: `{ totalDue: (record?.totalDue??0)+ext, totalPaid: (record?.totalDue??0)+ext, balance: 0 }`. The display and write logic use the same mathematical basis. There is no branching to maintain, no risk of per-case drift, and the test changes are fully enumerated. Option B produces identical runtime behavior with 3× the code. Option C is out of scope.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: `defaultCityTaxPerGuest` being in scope at the write site (confirmed: `ExtensionPayModal.tsx:71-74`); `saveCityTax` supporting node creation from `undefined` (confirmed: Firebase `update()` creates paths when absent).

rejected_approaches: **Option B (per-case branching)** — Functionally correct but produces 3 conditional branches where 1 unified write suffices. The extra branches triple the maintenance surface without adding any correctness benefit. Rejected on simplicity grounds.

**Option C (CityTaxPaymentButton helper delegation)** — Out of scope per fact-find non-goals. The helper would require `totalDue` to be incremented first as a separate step, pulling in `allFinancialTransactions` mirroring. Rejected for scope expansion.

validation_implications: All tests run in CI only (per `docs/testing-policy.md`)
TypeScript typecheck must pass locally before push
No migration or data cleanup needed
sequencing_constraints: Display fix and write fix can be implemented in one commit — they are in the same file
Test changes must follow or accompany the code changes
risks_to_carry_forward: `isSaving` guard already prevents double-submit; no additional guard needed

`updateBookingDates` is called before city tax write — if it throws, city tax is not written. This is pre-existing behavior, not a regression. Planning should note this is intentional (partial failure is surfaced via toast).

areas.label: City tax display
areas.current: `displayedCityTaxTotal` shows `record.balance` for case A guests — omits extension nights' amount
areas.trigger: Staff open extension modal with case A guest selected
areas.future: `displayedCityTaxTotal` returns `(record?.balance??0) + defaultCityTaxPerGuest` for all occupants in `cityTaxTargets`
areas.steady: Radio buttons, date extension logic, accommodation price display
areas.seams: None — purely additive to existing useMemo

areas.label: City tax write (case A — balance > 0)
areas.current: Zeroes balance but omits extension nights from `totalDue`
areas.trigger: Staff check "Mark city tax as paid" → click "Extend"
areas.future: `saveCityTax(bookingRef, id, { totalDue: oldDue+ext, totalPaid: oldDue+ext, balance: 0 })` → `saveActivity(id, { code: ActivityCode.CITY_TAX_PAYMENT })`
areas.seams: `saveCityTax` completes before `saveActivity` — if activity write fails, city tax record is persisted but activity is not logged; modal stays open (existing error-handling pattern, now applies to more occupants)

areas.label: City tax write (case B — balance = 0)
areas.seams: Same partial-write risk as case A — widened blast radius because case B occupants newly enter the `Promise.all` city-tax fan-out

areas.label: City tax write (case C — no record)
areas.seams: Same partial-write risk — case C occupants also newly enter the fan-out

areas.label: Inbox pricing (downstream read)
areas.future: No code change — zeroing `balance` on payment now consistently reduces inbox-displayed city tax due
areas.seams: None — already correct behavior; correctly benefits from the fix
